channel.title: TeslaBlog.eu — Tesla News & Updates
channel.description: Latest Tesla news, deals, and updates across Europe and beyond.

item.title: The Tesla Semi Might Get Automated And Wireless Charging
item.description: Two company executives told Jay Leno that the Semi could just drive over a pad to initiate a charging session in the future.

item.title: Tesla Finally Breaks Its 13-Month Sales Losing Streak in Europe
item.description: For a little over a year, Tesla’s might has been on the decline in Europe. That stopped in February.

item.title: Elon Musk’s Boring Co. Tunnel Vision Challenge ends with a surprise for Louisiana, Maryland and Dallas
item.description: Elon Musk’s The Boring Company (TBC) announced today that it is building free underground Loop tunnels in three American cities: New Orleans, Louisiana; Baltimore, Maryland; and Dallas, Texas. The company had promised one winner when it launched the Tunnel Vision Challenge in January. After recei...

item.title: Tesla launches first ‘true’ East Coast V4 Supercharger: here’s what that means
item.description: Tesla has launched its first “true” V4 Supercharger on the East Coast, and while that may be sort of confusing, here’s what we mean by that. Tesla has opened its first true V4 Supercharging station on the East Coast in Kissimmee, Florida, just south of Orlando. The eight-stall site, powered by an...

item.title: Tesla Semi has a million-mile battery, claims Tesla
item.description: Tesla Semi program lead Dan Priestley took Jay Leno on an extensive tour of the production-intent electric truck, revealing significant engineering details — including 1,000 lbs of weight savings that bring the 500-mile version to payload parity with diesel trucks. The episode is packed with new ...

item.title: Jay Leno Says The Tesla Semi’s Biggest Advantage Isn’t Its Range
item.description: Even though it can drive 500 miles on one charge, it’s the lower running costs that Jay believes will make this electric truck popular.

item.title: Tesla reveals various improvements to the Semi in new piece with Jay Leno
item.description: Tesla has revealed the various improvements it has made to the Semi with its redesign, which was unveiled late last year, on a new episode of Jay Leno’s Garage. Tesla Chief Designer Franz von Holzhausen and Semi Program Director Dan Priestley joined Leno in a 47-minute segment revealing all of th...

item.title: Jay Leno Drives the 500-Mile Tesla Semi
item.description: submitted by /u/4thAndLong [link] [comments]

item.title: Tesla patent reveals dual-battery system for trailer-mounted range extender
item.description: A newly published Tesla patent application describes a sophisticated dual-battery management system designed to integrate an auxiliary battery pack with a vehicle’s primary pack — including a configuration where the auxiliary battery lives inside a towed trailer. The patent was filed in August 20...

item.title: Tesla launches amazing new feature for shared vehicles
item.description: Tesla is launching an amazing new feature for shared vehicles, giving owners more transparency when they choose to have a Tesla ownership experience with another driver. This is one of the many advantages of having a Tesla. New features are constantly rolled out through software updates and Over-...

item.title: Elon Musk’s TERAFAB project: Everything you need to know
item.description: On Sunday, Elon Musk formally made TERAFAB official—a groundbreaking $20-25 billion joint venture uniting Tesla, SpaceX, and xAI, three of the world’s richest man’s most significant and powerful ventures. Musk described the project as “the most epic chip building exercise in history by far.” Elon...

item.title: Production Cybercab on display at the SXSW shows larger FSD cameras, interior details, ambient lighting, charge port improvements, and more
item.description: submitted by /u/CarCooler [link] [comments]

item.title: Elon Musk offers to pay TSA salaries as government shutdown leaves agents without paychecks
item.description: Elon Musk says that he is willing to personally cover the salaries of Transportation Security Administration (TSA) workers caught in the crossfire of a partial government shutdown that has now dragged on for over a month. “I would like to offer to pay the salaries of TSA personnel during this fun...

item.title: Tesla May Release Trailer With Battery
item.description: submitted by /u/ascottallison [link] [comments]

item.title: Tesla Bull Spends 4 Days In San Francisco Testing The Robotaxi
item.description: Tesla Robotaxi in San Francisco is cheaper than Waymo/Uber/Lyft, offers smooth comparable rides, but has longer waits and rare safety-driver interventions. Unsupervised rollout feels near with upcoming FSD update. Here at CleanTechnica, we have written a lot over the years on Tesla’s long path to...

item.title: Elon Musk launches TERAFAB: The $25B Tesla-SpaceXAI chip factory that will rewire the AI industry
item.description: Elon Musk took the stage over the weekend at the defunct Seaholm Power Plant in Austin, Texas, to officially unveil TERAFAB, a $20-25 billion joint venture between Tesla, SpaceX, and xAI that he described as “ the most epic chip building exercise in history by far .” The announcement marks the mo...

item.title: Tesla and SpaceX announce $25B ‘Terafab’ chip factory — here’s why it reeks of desperation
item.description: Tesla and SpaceX have unveiled “Terafab,” a joint $25 billion chip fabrication facility in Austin, Texas, that Elon Musk claims will produce 1 terawatt of computing power annually. It would be the largest semiconductor fab ever built — by an absurd margin. Musk took the stage at the defunct Seaho...

item.title: Why Lucid Is Cheering The End Of The Tesla Model S And Model X
item.description: When hundreds of thousands of Model S and Model X customers return to shop luxury EVs, they'll have plenty of choices.

item.title: Auto(nomous)bots, roll(er) out! The $1.75 BILLION future of self driving asphalt
item.description: Autonomous construction tech startup Bedrock Robotics has just closed on a $270 million Series B funding round that brings total investment in the self-driving roller and excavator firm to more than $350 million. (!) more…

item.title: Does The Tesla Semi Face Critical Market Challenges?
item.description: A week ago, I wrote an article pondering why the best news we get about the Tesla Semi, after nearly a decade, is still just small pilot program announcements. This was supposed to be a revolutionary semi truck that beat fossil-fueled semi trucks at almost everything and offered breakthrough impr...

item.title: Elon Musk teases expectations for Tesla’s AI6 self-driving chip
item.description: Tesla CEO Elon Musk is outlining expectations for the AI6 self-driving chip, which is still two generations away. Despite this, it is already in the plans of the company and its serial entrepreneur CEO, who has high expectations for it. Musk provided fresh details on the company’s aggressive AI h...

item.title: One-Pedal Driving Isn't A Safety Issue: Feds
item.description: Tesla marked safe from a recall over one-pedal driving that could have affected over 2 million cars.

item.title: Tesla’s Camera & Weather Problem Is Serious
item.description: I’m honestly surprised it has taken this long for a serious problem to surface related to Tesla’s Full Self Driving (FSD) system, based entirely around small cameras on the sensor side, and the weather. Having had a Tesla Model 3 with Full Self Driving (FSD) for more than 6 years, ... [continue...

item.title: A 24/7 solar farm-building robot just hit the market
item.description: The US needs a lot more electricity fast, and one company thinks robots can help build it quicker. Berkeley, California-based Terabase Energy says its next-gen Terafab automated solar construction system has finished field testing and is now ready to ship commercially. Terafab V2 brings autonomou...

item.title: Stellantis EVs Get Tesla Supercharger Access
item.description: It was Tesla’s biggest “mote” on the US EV market for years — its widespread, fantabulous, easy-to-use Supercharging network. Several years ago, Tesla offered to open it up to other automakers if they simply 1) helped fund it, and 2) made cars that were compatible. No one bit.

item.title: Podcast: Tesla turns to energy, BMW i3 EV makes a splash, Donut Lab update, and more
item.description: In the Electrek Podcast , we discuss the most popular news in the world of sustainable transport and energy. In this week’s episode, we discuss Tesla turning to energy, BMW i3 EV making a splash, Donut Lab update, and more. more…

item.title: SpaceX is quietly becoming the U.S. Military’s only reliable rocket
item.description: The U.S. Space Force announced today it is switching an upcoming GPS III satellite launch from United Launch Alliance’s Vulcan rocket to a SpaceX Falcon 9, a move that is as much a reflection of Vulcan’s mounting problems as it is a validation of SpaceX’s growing dominance in national security sp...

item.title: Tesla Full Self-Driving gets huge breakthrough on European expansion
item.description: Tesla Full Self-Driving has gotten a huge breakthrough as the company is still planning big things for its European expansion, hoping to bring the impressive platform into the continent after years of attempts. Tesla Europe has announced a major breakthrough : the company has officially completed...

item.title: NHTSA Elevates Tesla FSD Probe To Engineering Analysis
item.description: NHTSA has bumped up its investigation of the Tesla Full Self Driving software suite to an engineering analysis. The post NHTSA Elevates Tesla FSD Probe To Engineering Analysis appeared first on CleanTechnica .

item.title: Tesla delays FSD approval in Europe again, now expects April 10
item.description: Tesla’s European arm announced today that it has completed all vehicle testing for Full Self-Driving (Supervised) in the Netherlands — but the expected approval date has slipped yet again, from March 20 to April 10. The announcement, which Tesla framed as progress, also reveals that a broader EU-...

item.title: Tesla (TSLA) reportedly in talks to buy $2.9B in Chinese solar equipment for 100 GW US push
item.description: Elon Musk’s plan to build 100 GW of solar manufacturing capacity in the United States just got its first major price tag: $2.9 billion in equipment from Chinese suppliers, according to a Reuters exclusive. If the deal closes, it marks the biggest concrete investment yet in Musk’s solar ambitions,...

item.title: Tesla’s $2.9 billion bet: Why Elon Musk is turning to China to build America’s solar future
item.description: Tesla is reportedly in talks to purchase $2.9 billion worth of solar manufacturing equipment from a group of Chinese suppliers, including Suzhou Maxwell Technologies , which is the world’s largest producer of screen-printing equipment used in solar cell production. According to Reuters sources, t...

item.title: Elon Musk reveals date of Tesla Full Self-Driving’s next massive release
item.description: Tesla CEO Elon Musk revealed the date of Full Self-Driving’s next massive release: v14.3. For months, Tesla owners with Hardware 4 have been utilizing Full Self-Driving v14.2 and subsequent releases. Currently, the most up-to-date FSD version is v14.2.2.5, which has definitely brought out mixed r...

item.title: The last domino falls: Stellantis EVs now have Tesla Supercharger access
item.description: As of today, Jeep, Dodge and Stellantis EVs can plug in and charge on Tesla’s Supercharger network, the largest DC charging network in North America. Stellantis was the last brand set to gain access, so now just about every EV in America can use Superchargers, with the right adapters. more…

item.title: Chattanooga Charge: Tesla and EV fans ready for the Southeast’s wildest Tesla party
item.description: Hundreds of like-minded Tesla and EV enthusiasts are descending on Chattanooga Charge this weekend for the largest Tesla meet in the Southeast. Taking place on March 20–22, 2026 at the stunning Tennessee Riverpark. If you were there last year, you’ll know that it’s the ultimate experience to see ...

item.title: Tesla Full Self-Driving gets latest bit of scrutiny from NHTSA
item.description: The National Highway Traffic Safety Administration (NHTSA) has elevated its probe into Tesla’s Full Self-Driving (Supervised) suite to an Engineering Analysis. The analysis impacts roughly 3.2 million vehicles across the company’s entire lineup, and aims to identify how the suite’s degradation de...

item.title: Tesla is one stop away from having to recall FSD in NHTSA visibility crash probe
item.description: NHTSA has escalated its investigation into Tesla’s “Full Self-Driving” system’s inability to handle reduced visibility conditions, upgrading the probe to an Engineering Analysis covering an estimated 3,203,754 vehicles — the step that typically precedes a recall. The agency found that FSD’s degra...

item.title: Uber to invest over 1 billion dollars in Rivian to deploy tens of thousands of R2 robotaxis
item.description: Rideshare behemoth Uber announced a massive new partnership with Rivian, in which it will invest over one billion dollars in the American automaker to deploy up to 50,000 all-electric robotaxis, beginning with the new R2 SUV . more…

item.title: Xiaomi launches next-gen SU7 with 902 km range and LiDAR, still undercuts Tesla
item.description: Xiaomi is officially launching the next-generation SU7 electric sedan today with a sweeping set of upgrades — up to 902 km of CLTC range, standard LiDAR across all trims, and an 897V charging architecture on the top model. The updated sedan starts at 229,900 yuan (~$33,000), still undercutting th...

item.title: SpaceX’s Starship V3 is almost ready and it will change space travel forever
item.description: SpaceX is closing in on one of the most anticipated rocket launches in history, as the company readies for a planned April test launch and debut of its next-gen Starship V3 “Version 3”. The latest iteration of Starship V3 has a slightly taller Super Heavy booster and Starship upper stage than the...

item.title: Tesla Shines Amid EV Slowdown in China — February 2026 Sales Report
item.description: After the usual December EV record sales peak in China, which coincided with an end-of-incentive sales rush (NEVs are no longer exempt from purchase tax this year), and adding to the Chinese New Year happening in February, EVs had an expected sales slump, down by 32% — which sounds like ... [co...

item.title: Tesla Cybertruck gets long-awaited safety feature
item.description: Tesla is rolling out a new and long-awaited feature to the Cybertruck all-electric pickup, and it is a safety addition geared toward pedestrian and cyclist safety, as well as accidents with other vehicles. Tesla has announced the rollout of its innovative anti-dooring protection feature to the Cy...

item.title: Tesla says FSD was off before Cybertruck crash — but the video tells a different story
item.description: A viral dashcam video shows a Tesla Cybertruck slamming into a concrete overpass barrier on a Houston highway while allegedly using “Full Self-Driving.” Elon Musk responded by saying Tesla’s logs show the driver disengaged the system 4 seconds before impact. Tesla fans jumped on this as yet anoth...

item.title: Tesla Roadster is ‘sorcery and magic’ and might be worth the wait, Uber founder says
item.description: Tesla is planning to unveil the Roadster in late April after years of waiting. But the wait might be worth it, according to Travis Kalanick, the founder of Uber, who recently shed some light on his expectations for the all-electric supercar. We all know the Roadster is supposed to have some serio...

item.title: Former Uber CEO says Waymo ‘obviously’ ahead of Tesla in robotaxi race
item.description: Travis Kalanick, the former CEO of Uber who helped pioneer the ride-hailing industry, says Waymo is “obviously” ahead of Tesla in the robotaxi race, and that Elon Musk’s company needs a “ChatGPT moment” for its vision-based autonomous driving to catch up. The comments came during an appearance on...

item.title: Elon Musk clarifies viral Tesla Cybertruck accident with driver logs
item.description: Tesla CEO Elon Musk has clarified some details regarding the viral Tesla Cybertruck accident with company driver logs, which show various metrics at the time of an incident. The logs have been used in the past to pull responsibility off of Tesla when the automaker’s Full Self-Driving (Supervised)...

item.title: Washington opens Tesla’s direct EV sales loophole to Rivian and Lucid
item.description: Washington state legislators just voted overwhelmingly to allow Rivian and Lucid to sell electric vehicles directly to consumers, ending a 12-year arrangement that gave Tesla exclusive access to direct sales in the state. Senate Bill 6354 passed the House 84-9 and the Senate 47-2 — a bipartisan b...

item.title: Tesla Just Outsold Every Other Car Brand Combined in Norway
item.description: submitted by /u/InternetSolid4166 [link] [comments]

item.title: Tesla Model 3 Price Drop in Europe — Up to €4,000 Off with Tesla Bonus (March 2026)
item.description: Tesla has introduced the Tesla Bonus discount program in Europe, cutting Model 3 prices by up to €4,000 in select markets. Combined with referral code carlo719460 and government EV subsidies, the Model 3 can now be purchased for under €25,000 in some countries.

item.title: Tesla Robotaxi appears to be heading to a new U.S. city
item.description: Tesla Robotaxi appears to be heading to a new U.S. city, and although the company has revealed plans to launch in six new metros this year, it has yet to establish a new location outside of Austin and the Bay Area of California, where it has operated since last Summer. A lot full of Model Y vehic...
